issues.criterion: F-010/AT acceptance-criterion #3: 'tools/SKILL.md remains a router rather than a mirror'. Revision 2's fix (entry 13 widened to a whole-section closed-set check: bounded <=2-line/<=90-char preamble + exactly 4 contracted bullets + nothing else) is a genuine, tested improvement, but its own bounded PREAMBLE field is a free-text canvas constrained only by aggregate character length, with no word-count cap, no content-shape anchor, and no required-content check.
issues.evidence: On a disposable full-repository clone at the real, current HEAD 3aed5d1: replaced the honest 2-line preamble ('ecosystem/README.md is this module's own authoritative index...') with two DIFFERENT, independently-constructed 2-line preambles, each digesting all four protocols' operative rules, and re-ran entry 13 against both -- BOTH PASS. Variant 1 (81 + 89 = 170 chars across 2 lines): 'Preflight: verdict PASS/PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS/FAIL only; exceptions need sign-off.' / 'Baseline: one revision only. Audit: confidence<=evidence. Compare: deltas need a finding.' -- this literally reproduces the three exact preflight-verdict enum tokens (PASS/PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS/FAIL) practically verbatim, not merely a paraphrase. Variant 2 (76 + 81 = 157 chars): 'Preflight halts on FAIL; PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS needs recorded sign-off first.' / 'Audit confidence never exceeds evidence; Compare needs a named finding per delta.' -- also reproduces two of the three verdict enum tokens verbatim. Both variants: (a) pass entry 13 outright ('SECTION 8 IS A CLOSED SET'); (b) pass entry 15 (verbatim-canary), since neither reuses any of the four full canary SENTENCES (only shorter enum tokens, which the canary check does not inspect for); (c) pass entry 16 (40-line cap) and entry 12 (content-completeness) trivially. This is a genuine, quantified defeat of the SAME acceptance criterion the contract has now been rejected for twice already, via a THIRD distinct attack shape (preamble smuggling, as opposed to round 0's aggregate-mirror and round 1's per-bullet-stretch/free-paragraph).
issues.required_fix: Apply the same anti-paraphrase discipline the fix already applies to bullet labels to the preamble field: (a) PREFERRED -- pin the preamble to an exact, verbatim-matched string (or a small enumerated set of near-identical acceptable renderings), since the preamble's semantic role is fixed and narrow (name ecosystem/README.md as the module's authoritative index; state this skill remains the sole router) and, unlike the four bullets, does not need to vary per protocol -- an exact-text check closes this vector entirely at zero cost to legitimate design flexibility (design_notes.router_vs_mirror_design's stated reason for preferring a length bound over exact-text pinning -- 'more robust to legitimate minor rewording' -- does not outweigh the demonstrated cost: a length-only bound admits verbatim reproduction of exact protocol enum vocabulary). (b) IF exact-text pinning is rejected, at minimum add a word-count cap (not merely a character cap -- my two working variants used dense, symbol-heavy compression such as ':' and '/' and '<=' to pack far more propositional content per character than natural prose achieves) AND a keyword-based semantic-leakage guard that rejects any preamble line containing protocol-specific normative vocabulary (e.g. the literal strings 'PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS', 'FAIL', 'MUST', or any of the four protocols' own verdict/scoring terms) outside of the required 'ecosystem/README.md' mention.

missing_acceptance_coverage: The preamble's own REQUIRED CONTENT (that it must name ecosystem/README.md as the module's authoritative index and state this skill remains the router) is asserted only in prose (design_notes.router_vs_mirror_design and scope.files_modify's purpose text) but is not verified by entry 13 or any other verification entry -- entry 13 checks only the preamble's LINE COUNT and PER-LINE CHARACTER LENGTH, never its content. Demonstrated concretely: both preamble-smuggling fixtures above replace the required content entirely (neither mentions 'ecosystem/README.md' or 'router' at all) and both still pass. This is a distinct, lower-severity coverage gap from the mirroring issue above (it is about a missing POSITIVE requirement, not an exploitable negative one), but is related: a required-content check on the preamble would, as a side effect, also narrow the free-text canvas available for smuggling.
observations: ROUND-1 FIXTURES RE-VERIFIED TO NOW FAIL, AS CLAIMED: reproduced this evaluator's own round-1 18-line trailing-paragraph fixture (the exact text that defeated revision 1) verbatim against the new entry 13 -- correctly FAILS, flagged 'non-blank, non-bullet line found after bullets began'. Also independently constructed a FRESH attack shape not previously tested -- a paraphrase paragraph interleaved BETWEEN two bullets (after Preflight, before Baseline) rather than after all four -- entry 13 correctly FAILS this too, via the same 'found after bullets began' branch (once bullet_mode is set true by the first bullet, any subsequent non-bullet content anywhere, including between bullets, is flagged). This confirms the closed-set design genuinely treats the whole post-preamble region uniformly, not merely the tail.

EXACT-CARDINALITY AND ORDER, RE-VERIFIED WITH FRESH FIXTURES: a duplicate bullet (Preflight's path listed twice, 5 bullets total) correctly FAILS ('expected exactly 4 bullets, found 5', with the duplicate path visible in the reported list). A reordered bullet list (Baseline before Preflight, all four still present) correctly FAILS ('bullet order/path mismatch') on both displaced entries. An unauthorized fifth bullet (routing to ecosystem/README.md, forbidden by non_goals) correctly FAILS ('expected exactly 4 bullets, found 5') -- the round-1 MEDIUM finding is now genuinely closed, not merely re-labeled.

PARAPHRASE-BEFORE-PREAMBLE (OVER-BUDGET) PROBE: an attempt to replace the entire preamble with two paraphrase sentences resulted in one line exceeding the 90-character cap (102 chars) and was correctly caught by the length check alone -- this particular probe did not require the closed-set logic to fire, illustrating that the char cap DOES bind sufficiently dense single-line paraphrase attempts; the HIGH finding above only succeeds because it distributes the same propositional content across BOTH available lines and uses terser, symbol-heavy phrasing within each line's individual 90-character budget, rather than one long single-line attempt.

COMPARATIVE JUDGMENT AGAINST THE ROUND-1 LABEL-BUDGET RESIDUAL (explicitly requested): the round-1 label residual (a 4-6 word / 24-40 character gloss attached to ONE bullet at a time, e.g. 'confidence bounded by evidence') was judged non-blocking because it is categorically smaller in payload -- isolated per-protocol, generic wording, no exact enum vocabulary, and inherently shape-constrained by the bullet regex itself (a label is structurally a short trailing annotation, not a freestanding statement). The preamble residual found this round is judged MORE SEVERE and BLOCKING for three concrete, evidenced reasons: (1) it is not per-bullet-isolated -- a single 2-line block can synthesize all four protocols' rules together into one coherent digest, which is qualitatively closer to a genuine mini-summary than four independent tags; (2) it reproduces literal, exact protocol vocabulary (the verdict enum tokens PASS/PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS/FAIL) rather than a vague paraphrase -- this is arguably no longer even 'paraphrase' for that portion, it is verbatim terminology reuse the canary check does not catch because the canary only inspects four specific long SENTENCES, not shorter enum tokens; (3) unlike the label field, which is inherently shape-constrained by the enclosing bullet regex to read as a short trailing tag, the preamble has NO shape constraint at all -- it is an open two-line canvas whose only defense is aggregate length, exactly the defense mechanism (a length-only bound) that round 0 already proved structurally insufficient to distinguish label from mirror content. The same category of defect (a length-only bound applied to free natural-language text) that was fixed for bullets in revision 1 has now simply reappeared, unfixed, in the preamble field introduced by revision 2's own remedy.

FULL 26-ENTRY SUITE + validate.sh, RE-VERIFIED AT THE REAL, CURRENT, COMMITTED HEAD 3aed5d1: built a fresh, independently-authored, fully honest 3-surface edit (distinct wording from the generator's own fixtures) on a clean disposable clone and ran all 26 of contract 040's own verification commands verbatim, in array order: 26/26 exit 0 PASS, including the widened entry 13. `bash tools/validate.sh` full sweep: 'SUMMARY: 11 passed, 0 failed'.

SCOPE / NON_GOALS / ESTIMATED_LINES: diffed revision 2 against revision 1 -- 'scope.files_modify' paths unchanged (NIZAM.json, tools/skill.json, tools/SKILL.md), only the tools/SKILL.md purpose text updated to describe the closed-set/bounded-preamble design (expected, self-consistent with the entry-13 rewrite). 'non_goals' grew from 14 to 15 items -- the new 15th item ('No content in tools/SKILL.md's new Section 8 beyond the bounded preamble ... and the exactly four contracted routing bullets') is the expected, intentional codification of the closed-set fix, not unauthorized scope creep; the prior 14 items are otherwise verbatim. 'estimated_lines' (95) unchanged. The verification array grew from 26 (no -- from 26 in revision 1 to 26 again in revision 2; the widened entry 13 replaced the prior per-bullet-only entry 13 in place, no net change in array length) -- confirmed by direct count.

PROCESS NOTE -- CIRCUIT BREAKER: this is the THIRD CONSECUTIVE rejection of contract 040's negotiation (round 0: aggregate-line-cap gameable by a full multi-line paraphrase-mirror; round 1/revision 1: the per-bullet-only fix left every other line in the section, including a trailing free paragraph, completely uninspected; round 2/revision 2, this review: the whole-section closed-set fix's own bounded preamble field is itself a length-only-constrained free-text canvas that reproduces exact protocol vocabulary). Three attempts, three genuinely distinct root causes, per CLAUDE.md's universal 'Circuit Breaker (3-Strike Limit)' ('After three failed attempts on any single step: 1. HALT execution 2. Mark the step as BLOCKED 3. Log the failure in .agent/run_state.json and docs/planning/DEBT.md 4. Escalate to the human for review 5. Attempt 4+ is FORBIDDEN' absent explicit human authorization, mirroring the .agent/run_state.json precedent recorded for features 027 and 028). This evaluator does not have write permission to docs/planning/DEBT.md (READ-ONLY per this role's Permission Boundaries) or to .agent/run_state.json (orchestrator-owned); this circuit-breaker trigger is reported here, in this artifact's observations, and in this evaluator's final chat-facing report, for the orchestrator to log and to escalate to the human before authorizing any further (fourth) contract-negotiation round on feature 040.

PROBE ISOLATION CONFIRMED: all probes in this round-3 review ran against disposable `cp -a` full-repository clones under the session scratchpad directory, outside the real repository tree, plus read-only `git log`/`git status` inspection of the real repository at /home/cnross/workspace02/nizam-framework. Nothing under ecosystem/, schema/, tools/, docs/, NIZAM.json, or any .agent path other than this review's own artifact (.agent/qa/040-contract-review-rev2.json) was created, edited, or deleted in the real repository.

final_verdict.summary: NOT approved at revision 2. Revision 2's whole-section closed-set widening of entry 13 correctly and verifiably closes every attack shape from the round-1 rejection: this evaluator's own 18-line trailing-paragraph fixture now fails, a fresh between-bullets-interleaved variant fails, duplicate bullets fail (exact-cardinality), reordered bullets fail (order assertion), and the round-1 MEDIUM unauthorized-fifth-bullet gap is genuinely closed. However, the fix introduces a NEW, unaddressed gap in the same acceptance criterion: the closed-set check's own bounded PREAMBLE field (<=2 lines, <=90 characters each) has no word-count cap, no content-shape anchor, and no required-content check -- only an aggregate-length bound, which round 0 already proved structurally insufficient to separate router content from mirror content. Two independently-constructed 2-line preambles (170 and 157 characters respectively, well within the 180-character theoretical maximum) each digest all four protocols' operative rules -- including literal reproduction of the exact preflight verdict enum tokens PASS/PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS/FAIL, not merely a paraphrase -- and both pass entry 13, entry 15 (verbatim-canary, which only inspects four specific long sentences, not shorter enum tokens), entry 16 (line-cap), and entry 12 (content-completeness) simultaneously. Judged against this evaluator's own round-1 standard (where a comparable but smaller residual -- compressed per-bullet labels -- was accepted as non-blocking): this preamble residual is judged MORE severe and BLOCKING, because it is not per-item-isolated (a single block synthesizes all four protocols together), it reproduces literal enum vocabulary rather than vague gist, and -- critically -- the preamble has no inherent shape constraint the way a bullet's regex-anchored '- `path` -- label' format does; it is exactly the kind of open, length-bounded natural-language canvas that has now caused this same acceptance criterion to be defeated in three consecutive rounds via three genuinely different mechanisms. Required before re-approval: extend the fix's own logic to the preamble -- either pin it to exact, verbatim text (preferred, since its role is fixed and narrow and does not need per-protocol variability), or at minimum add a word-count cap plus a keyword-based semantic-leakage guard. Separately and non-blocking: the preamble's own required positive content (naming ecosystem/README.md, asserting router-not-mirror) is not verified anywhere, only its length. Every other mechanism reviewed in rounds 0-1 remains sound at the real, current HEAD 3aed5d1, and the full 26-entry verification suite plus the full validate.sh sweep were independently reproduced as 100% passing for a genuinely honest edit. PROCESS FLAG: this is the third consecutive contract-review rejection for feature 040 with three distinct root causes, which trips CLAUDE.md's universal 3-strike Circuit Breaker; a fourth negotiation round should not proceed without explicit human authorization, logged by the orchestrator to .agent/run_state.json and docs/planning/DEBT.md (outside this evaluator's write permissions).
